varieties.name: Massey Gem 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Dark green, bold seeds; early maturing
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer with good disease resistance
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, various Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Telephone (Alderman / Tall Telephone) 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: England, Carter's of London, 1878 (as Telephone); introduced to USA as Alderman by W. Atlee Burpee, 1901
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Large, dark green pods 10-13cm (4-5 inches) long; 8-10 peas per pod
varieties.yield_notes: Very heavy cropper with extended harvest period; enormous crops of large, well-filled pods
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, The Diggers Club, wide availability in Australia

varieties.name: Greenfeast 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Green, smooth-seeded cultivar; pods 5.5-6.5cm long
varieties.availability: Eden Seeds, widely available from Australian seed companies

varieties.name: Champion of England 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: England, introduced by William Fairbeard, 1843 (originally Fairbeard's Champion of England)
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Long pods 10cm; 8-10 large peas per pod; white flowers; elegant silver-marked leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy yields; long pods well-filled; continued production well into summer
varieties.availability: Available from international heirloom suppliers; rare in Australia but can be sourced

varieties.name: Oregon Giant (Snow Pea) 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: USA, Oregon State University, Dr. James Baggett, released 1991
varieties.traits: Snow pea / edible pod; Large, flat pods 11-13cm (4.5-5 inches) long; thick, dark green pods; stringless
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding; abundant crops; early producing; extended harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to powdery mildew, pea enation mosaic virus (PEMV), and fusarium wilt race 1

varieties.name: Oregon Sugar Pod (Snow Pea) 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: USA, Oregon State University breeding program (1954+)
varieties.traits: Snow pea / edible pod; Flat edible pods to 10cm; dark green
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to pea enation mosaic virus, common wilt, powdery mildew

varieties.name: Yakumo (Snow Pea) 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.traits: Snow pea / edible pod; Large, light green flat pods 13cm long; pods do not cup

varieties.name: Sugar Snap 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: USA, 1979, created by Calvin Lamborn (cross between shelling pea mutant and snow pea)
varieties.traits: Sugar snap pea / edible pod; Thick-walled rounded pods 7.5cm (3 inches) long; crispy, crunchy texture
varieties.availability: Widely available from Australian seed companies

varieties.name: Sugar Ann (Sugar Snap) 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.traits: Sugar snap pea / edible pod; Crisp, sweet, thick-walled pods 7.5cm (3 inches) long
varieties.yield_notes: Early and productive; ready 10-14 days ahead of original Sugar Snap; remains in prime condition for days
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, various Australian suppliers

varieties.name: Sugar Bush (Sugarsnap Sugar Bush) 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.traits: Sugar snap pea / edible pod; Succulent, pale-green edible pods 7.6cm long

varieties.name: Pigeon Pea (Cajanus cajan) 🌱 PERENNIAL
varieties.origin: India, domesticated at least 3,500 years ago
varieties.traits: Perennial legume shrub (NOT true garden pea but related); Yellow/red legume flowers; pods with 4-5 seeds; trifoliate leaves (green top, grey bottom)
varieties.yield_notes: Can begin flowering within a few months
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Local Seeds Queensland, Eden Seeds

varieties.name: Arka Chaitra 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.origin: India, ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Long, light green colored pods; light green, round seeds
varieties.availability: ICAR-IIHR, Indian agricultural universities, tropical monsoon climates agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Arka Karthik 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.origin: India, ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Long, dark green colored pods; dark green, round seeds
varieties.availability: ICAR-IIHR, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, regional agricultural centers

varieties.name: Arka Prasad 🧬 HYBRID
varieties.origin: India, ICAR-Indian Institute of Horticultural Research
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Short, dark green colored pods; dark green, round seeds
varieties.availability: ICAR-IIHR, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University

varieties.name: Ooty 1 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: India, Ooty (Udhagamandalam), Tamil Nadu hill station variety
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Early wrinkled seeded variety; dwarf type
varieties.availability: Indian seed companies, Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University

varieties.name: Arkel 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.growing_notes: Early maturity helps evade powdery mildew
varieties.availability: Widely available from Indian seed companies, tropical monsoon climates agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Jawahar Matar Series 🌱 OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Wrinkled, yellow to green seeds; medium-sized
varieties.growing_notes: JM 4, JM 5, and JM 83 resistant to powdery mildew
varieties.availability: JNKVV (Jawahar Nehru Krishi Vishwa Vidyalaya), Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Wando 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: USA, Southeastern Vegetable Breeding Laboratory, released 1943 (cross between 'Laxton's Progress' and 'Perfection')
varieties.traits: Shelling pea; Short, dark green shelling pods; 7-8 peas per pod; medium-sized peas
varieties.growing_notes: Reasonably resistant to drought and root knot nematodes

varieties.name: Pigeon Pea / Red Gram (Cajanus cajan) 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Africa/Asia, widely cultivated in tropical monsoon climates for generations
varieties.traits: Warm-season legume (various types: bush, climbing, yard-long bean); Various seed colors: white, cream, brown, red, black with characteristic 'eye' marking
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout tropical monsoon climates from local agricultural suppliers, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, farmer cooperatives
